scope.family: finite stabilizer EPR-pairing resources with named left/right mouth ports
scope.minimality_scope: permutation EPR resources; first fixed-port transmission split occurs at m=2

core_claim: Coarse L/R entropy and min-cut count EPR resources but do not determine the named bridge channel; algebraic connectivity does for the finite permutation-EPR family.

theorem_style_claims.claim: For a permutation pi of EPR edges L_i--R_pi(i), the number of perfect named-port product channels under the identity decoder equals the number of fixed points of pi; this is not a quantum-capacity formula.
For fixed m, all permutation-EPR resources have the same coarse L/R entropy and min-cut shadow, while perfect fixed-port transmission counts vary.
The crossed m=2 identity decoder is the correlated II/XX/YY/ZZ twirl with a classical center and no noiseless qubit; pairing-aware decoding restores the exact identity channel.

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the finite bridge-channel benchmark certificate.
Regression check for the Goal 10 certificate.

diagnostic_hierarchy.port_resolved_entropy: distinguishes the simple EPR pairing witness
diagnostic_hierarchy.algebraic_connectivity_matrix: sufficient for the declared finite channel family

known_vs_new.conceptual_prior_art: Engelhardt-Liu 2023, Algebraic ER=EPR and Complexity Transfer, is the primary prior art for the broad operator-algebraic ER=EPR framing.
known_vs_new.known_derived: Standard stabilizer EPR teleportation, Choi matrices, and Clifford Pauli transfer.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages the finite bridge-channel certificate layer as an explicit simultaneous CPTP benchmark and records the correction from fixed-port transmission to preserved operator algebra.

limitations: This is a stabilizer port benchmark and exact wrong-mouth/permutation-scrambling proxy, not a continuum-gravity theorem, not a generic chaotic many-body traversable-wormhole simulation, and not a separation from full port-resolved entropy data.
next_frontier: Search non-Clifford or tensor-network families where complete entropy data collide while operator algebra predicts an operational recovery distinction.
